description: A container made of woven material; a receptacle of interlaced fibers. 'Wiru' names what hands weave—plant becoming pattern, pattern becoming purpose.
sound_symbolism: The word weaves connection. 'Wi' pairs in relationship, strands meeting. Then 'ru' curves around, basket taking shape through repetition.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo wiru' (the basket). Implies woven material—reed, willow, palm. Related to weaving as fundamental craft.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Basketry as first technology—weaving predates pottery, creating form from found materials.
pillars.solarpunk-values: Natural materials—baskets from local plants, biodegradable, infinitely renewable.
